chunks.text: My grandfather is eighty - four.
He began to paint ten years ago.
After retirement, he had time on his hands and wanted to focus on something.
In his youth, he had been convinced that he had no talent for painting.
A grammar teacher in middle school told him, " You are bad at painting.
You are better suited for music.
" Since then, he had not touched a brush.
But after retirement
he happened to stand before a painting in a museum and thought he wanted to paint too.
Already seventy - four, the family worried that he was too late to begin.
Yet he joined a local painting class.
People of various ages came to the class, but he was the oldest.
At first he was shy, but he gradually found joy in it.
Ten years on, his works hang on the walls throughout the house.
He has even held a small exhibition at a local gallery.
His grandchildren treasure his paintings as their favorite.
He says, " Age has nothing to do with beginning something.
Creation keeps a person young.
My grandfather's hand, when he holds a brush, does not tremble.
The act of painting calms his nerve and sharpens his focus.
Art may be the best solution to old age.
It is never too late to begin something.
My grandfather's figure demonstrates this to me.
